tagline: LA's favorite corn prep, in a bowl — smoky, creamy, and heat-forward
trend_signal: elote avg_12m 16.9, recent_4w 20.8 — rising trend in CA
review_evidence: Gracias Madre reviewer: 'I went with the esquites as my main, and oh my corn — it was next level. Roasted just right, kissed with poblano and jalapeño, and brought to life with chipotle aioli and brazil nut cotija... Every bite was smoky, creamy, spicy, crunchy.' | Another reviewer: 'We had the elote and guacamole as appetizers... all of the food here blew me away.'
gap_rationale: Gracias Madre's esquites/elote is a signature crowd-pleaser at WeHo's top plant-based Mexican spot. Chipotle's corn salsa, fajita veggies, guacamole, sour cream, and chipotle honey vinaigrette can approximate the smoky-creamy-spicy profile locals crave without needing cotija or crema.
confidence_reason: Pantry can build the flavor direction with existing corn salsa + sour cream + chipotle honey vinaigrette. However, no cotija or charred corn format — this approximates elote rather than authentically delivering it. Flag: true elote needs cotija/crema (see GAP below).

review_evidence: Gracias Madre reviewer: 'smoky, creamy, spicy, crunchy — basically everything your taste buds didn't know they needed' describing esquites with chipotle aioli and brazil nut cotija. Second reviewer ordered elote as a standalone appetizer and 'blew me away.'
local_evidence: Gracias Madre owns the WeHo elote moment — it's name-checked in multiple reviews as a must-order. The off-cob esquites format with a creamy cheese topping is what drives repeat visits. Chipotle's corn salsa is too mild/fresh to deliver this.
solution: Add cotija crumble as a topping SKU and a charred corn preparation option. Mexican crema as an alternative to sour cream would also close the gap cleanly.

tagline: Smoky-sweet Korean-Mex crossover — the WeHo fusion moment locals are already eating
trend_signal: kimchi avg_12m 53.4, recent_4w 54.8 rising in CA; bulgogi avg_12m 22.0, recent_4w 22.5 rising
review_evidence: Moonbowls reviewer: 'AhMazing Korean fried chicken dusted in the most delicious seasonings... All gluten free.' | Another Moonbowls reviewer: 'I built my own bowl and added tofu, beyond beef, kimchi and mushrooms... such a hearty salad I was full mid salad.' | Ghost kitchen reviewer at Moonbowls: 'The Jerk Chicken Burrito is outstanding! The tanginess of the cabbage slaw and the pickled onions with the sweetness of the coconut rice... the portion is large and a good value.'
gap_rationale: Moonbowls WeHo and its ghost kitchen tenant demonstrate that Korean-Mexican burrito fusion already has a hungry audience steps from Chipotle's Beverly location. The existing adobo chicken, corn salsa, and tomatillo salsa can lean into the smoky-sweet lane.
confidence_reason: Chipotle already has a Saucy KBBQ Chicken wrap in Sweetgreen's format showing market proof. Pantry supports the build; the gap is gochujang/sesame sauce not in Chipotle's pantry. Works as a limited promotion with existing ingredients approximating the flavor.

review_evidence: Moonbowls reviewer: 'Very flavorful - really liked the kimchi and marinated cucumber sides.' | Another: 'I've only had delivery, but it's always hot and delicious. I miss the gochujang sweet potatoes, but it's forced me to try other good stuff.'
local_evidence: Moonbowls WeHo has built its entire identity around Korean bowl flavors — kimchi, gochujang, bulgogi — and is walkable from Chipotle 8420 Beverly. The demand is proven; Chipotle has zero Korean pantry items.
solution: Add kimchi as a topping SKU and a gochujang-sesame sauce as a LTO dressing. Both are increasingly standard in fast-casual and require minimal supply chain complexity.

tagline: The WeHo Mediterranean moment — harissa lamb, fire-roasted corn, sumac slaw on saffron rice
trend_signal: harissa avg_12m 7.1, recent_4w 7.2 rising in CA; sumac avg_12m 11.3 rising; shakshuka avg_12m 7.2 rising
review_evidence: Open Sesame reviewer: 'Most amazing garlic dip, hummus and the lamb is cooked perfectly.' | Another: 'Every dish was packed with flavor... Spicy hummus - creamy and full of flavor, 5/5. Falafel - crispy on the outside, soft inside, 5/5.' | K'Far-parallel reviewer on shakshuka: 'the shakshuka was unique bc the eggs were very smoky... I liked it a lot.'
gap_rationale: Open Sesame (Beverly/Fairfax) is driving strong demand for North African-Lebanese spice profiles — harissa lamb, bold garlic, fresh herbs — in CAVA's exact price band. CAVA's pantry is already built for this with lamb meatballs, harissa, roasted eggplant, and tahini.
confidence_reason: All ingredients are confirmed CAVA pantry items. Harissa lamb meatballs + harissa + sumac slaw + fire-roasted corn + lemon herb tahini is a fully authentic build. Trend signals rising. Local competitor validates demand at accessible price point.

tagline: Plant-forward, spiced, roasted — for the WeHo crowd who loves Crossroads but wants a $15 lunch
trend_signal: cauliflower avg_12m 70.5, recent_4w 69.0 rising in CA; lentils avg_12m 46.7 rising
review_evidence: Crossroads Kitchen reviewer: 'The rigatoni was a standout... perfectly seasoned and spicy with the chicken still having that deliciously addictive crunch. The mushroom arancini were also delicious!' | Open Sesame: 'You absolutely MUST order the spicy garlic potatoes and the flash fried cauliflower. I was blown away — the cauliflower were perfectly crisp.' | Moonbowls: 'Moonbowls are quick and easy, with big flavors that pack a punch. I usually get the BBQ Chicken Bowl or Steak Bowl with extra fried cauliflower.'
gap_rationale: Flash-fried/roasted cauliflower is emerging as a WeHo star dish across multiple restaurants. CAVA's roasted vegetable protein + fiery broccoli + lentils + hummus creates a genuinely plant-forward build that the Crossroads/Gracias Madre crowd would recognize at fast-casual speed.
confidence_reason: Fully supported by CAVA pantry. Cauliflower is in the roasted vegetables protein. Trend signal strong. Multiple local restaurants have made cauliflower a signature — CAVA can own it at scale.

review_evidence: Open Sesame reviewer: 'Zaatar pizza - light, fresh, and flavorful, 5/5.' | K'Far (Williamsburg parallel): 'the shakshuka was very smoky and unlike anything I've had... very rich.'
local_evidence: Open Sesame WeHo has made za'atar a signature — appearing on flatbreads, salads, and as a seasoning across the menu. CAVA's Mediterranean identity makes it a natural fit but za'atar is completely absent from the pantry.
solution: Add za'atar as a dry seasoning topping SKU (low cost, shelf stable). A tomato-harissa warm base could also support a shakshuka-inspired bowl with existing harissa and eggs.

review_evidence: Gracias Madre reviewer raved about 'the lentil-quinoa barbacoa taco salad with herb jalapeño dressing' — mole-adjacent depth. While mole wasn't explicitly name-dropped at WeHo Yelp spots, the trend score (76.0, rising) is the highest Mexican-adjacent signal in CA.
local_evidence: Mole is the highest-trending Mexican flavor term in CA (76.0 avg, 76.0 recent — peak 100). No WeHo fast-casual brand touches it. Gap is enormous but pantry requirements are prohibitive.
solution: CAVA specifically cannot close this gap without 30+ dried chile varieties and chocolate. Flag to culinary team for a dedicated mole-spiced protein marinade development, not a topping add. Multi-quarter SKU effort.

tagline: Sweetgreen already has the build — WeHo locals are eating this at Moonbowls right now
ingredients: wrapped in our new olive oil tortilla
trend_signal: kimchi avg_12m 53.4, recent_4w 54.8 rising; bulgogi avg_12m 22.0, recent_4w 22.5 rising in CA
review_evidence: Moonbowls WeHo: 'What a TREAT!! Being a vegetarian who likes "stuff in my salads" moonbowl was incredible! I built my own bowl and added tofu, beyond beef, kimchi and mushrooms... such a hearty salad.' | Another: 'AhMazing Korean fried chicken dusted in the most delicious seasonings as well as the French fries with dipping sauces. All gluten free.'
gap_rationale: Sweetgreen's Saucy KBBQ Chicken wrap already exists in the menu — this is about positioning it explicitly for the WeHo Korean-bowl demand that Moonbowls is capturing. The pantry is fully confirmed.
confidence_reason: The Saucy KBBQ Chicken wrap is a confirmed menu item. Apple kimchi sauce and kbbq dressing are in the pantry. Moonbowls WeHo validates the local appetite. This is a marketing/placement play more than an ingredient add.

tagline: Crossroads Kitchen's $40 lion's mane moment — Sweetgreen's portobello mix gets the same reverence
trend_signal: cauliflower avg_12m 70.5 rising; lentils avg_12m 46.7 rising — plant-forward trend strong in CA
review_evidence: Crossroads Kitchen reviewer: 'The standout for me was definitely the Lion's Mane mushroom dish - the texture and flavor were incredible, and I actually preferred it over the eggplant steak. The Lion's Mane had a richer, more satisfying bite.' | Another: 'This is way overdue... the all stars are Impossible cigars, squash blossoms, vodka pasta, mushroom pizza, and lion's mane steak.'
gap_rationale: Crossroads Kitchen has made lion's mane mushroom the prestige plant protein of WeHo. Sweetgreen's portobello mix is the closest analog in the Sweetgreen pantry — the Shroomami bowl already exists. The opportunity is framing the portobello build as the premium mushroom moment for plant-based diners priced out of Crossroads.
confidence_reason: Portobello mix is in pantry, all other Harvest Bowl ingredients confirmed. True lion's mane mushroom is not in pantry — this is the closest authentic build available. Positioning opportunity is real; pantry gap from Crossroads standard is real.

review_evidence: Gracias Madre reviewers repeatedly praised 'the tacos (all flavors)' and 'jackfruit carnitas burrito' — the slow-braised, deeply-spiced protein tradition. One reviewer specifically sought 'the asada burrito' expecting deeper flavor than received.
local_evidence: Birria is the second-highest trending Mexican term in WeHo CA after mole (52.0 recent, rising vs 46.6 avg). No fast-casual brand in the area offers it. The dried chile base makes it a genuine pantry gap — not achievable with Sweetgreen's current ingredients.
solution: Braised beef or mushroom birria protein as a new LTO protein SKU with consomme-style dipping sauce. Requires supply chain addition of dried guajillo/ancho chiles. Partner with a local birria supplier (LA has dozens) for authentic sourcing story.
